methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: Total supply must remain strictly constant during all transfer operations, including fee deductions and gasless base fee payments.
invariants.attack_scenario: A math error or missing state update in the complex fee deduction logic could inadvertently mint or burn tokens, breaking the stablecoin's 1:1 fiat backing.

invariants.description: A minter's allowance must strictly decrease by the exact minted amount, unless they are a MASTER_MINTER.
invariants.attack_scenario: A compromised minter bypasses the allowance check or exploits an underflow, minting infinite tokens and causing hyperinflation.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_minterAllowanceDecreases() public { uint256 pre = minterAllowed[msg.sender]; mint(to, amount); assert(hasRole(MASTER_MINTER, msg.sender) || minterAllowed[msg.sender] == pre - amount); }

invariants.description: The sender's balance must decrease by exactly the transfer amount plus the calculated transaction fee, provided the fee faucet is set.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the fee is deducted from the recipient or not deducted at all despite being calculated, the protocol loses revenue or users can extract value.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_feeDeductionCorrectness() public { uint256 pre = balanceOf(sender); transfer(to, amount); uint256 expectedFee = _feesFaucet != address(0) ? calculateTxFee(amount) : 0; assert(balanceOf(sender) == pre - amount - expectedFee); }

invariants.description: A user executing a meta-transaction via the Forwarder must have their balance reduced by exactly the transfer amount, the transaction fee, and the gasless base fee.
invariants.attack_scenario: Relayers overcharge users for gasless transactions, draining their balances, or fees are bypassed, depriving the protocol of revenue.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_forwarderCostConservation() public { uint256 pre = balanceOf(req.from); execute(req, ...); uint256 expectedFee = _feesFaucet != address(0) ? calculateTxFee(req.value) : 0; assert(balanceOf(req.from) == pre - req.value - expectedFee - _gaslessBasefee); }

invariants.description: Burning tokens must not refund or increase a minter's allowance.
invariants.attack_scenario: A minter repeatedly mints and burns to manipulate supply metrics while bypassing their lifetime minting limit, treating allowance as a revolving credit line instead of a hard cap.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_burnDoesNotRefundAllowance() public { uint256 pre = minterAllowed[msg.sender]; burn(amount); assert(minterAllowed[msg.sender] == pre); }

invariants.description: The token contract itself must never receive its own tokens.
invariants.attack_scenario: Users accidentally sending tokens to the contract address lose them forever, or an admin mistakenly sets the fee faucet to the token contract, trapping protocol revenue.

invariants.description: A blacklisted address must never be able to receive tokens, even via admin forced transfers.
invariants.attack_scenario: A sanctioned entity receives tokens through a forced transfer or complex meta-transaction, violating compliance and risking regulatory shutdown.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_blacklistedCannotReceive() public view { assert(!isBlacklisted(to) || revertExpected); }
